title: Lists & Basic Operations
objective: Create and manipulate Python lists for storing sequences of data.

exercise.description: Store daily step counts for 7 days. Compute total and average. Given a list of marks, remove the lowest mark and compute the new average.
exercise.solution:
```python
steps = [5000, 7200, 4500, 8000, 6000, 5500, 9000]
total = sum(steps)
avg = total / len(steps)
print(f'Total: {total}, Average: {avg:.0f}')

marks = [85, 72, 90, 68, 88]
marks.remove(min(marks))
new_avg = sum(marks) / len(marks)
print(f'New average (lowest removed): {new_avg:.1f}')
```

homework: What is the difference between list.append(x) and list.insert(0, x)? Give examples.

problems.q: Reverse a list using slicing (without reverse())
Get every second element from a list starting at index 0

example2.title: List methods
example2.code:
lst = [1, 2, 3]
lst.append(4)
lst.insert(0, 0)
print(lst)
x = lst.pop()
print(x, lst)

application: Lists store sequences: time series data, feature vectors, batches of samples. Slicing extracts subsets for analysis.
caseStudy: A sensor records temperatures every hour. The last 24 values are kept in a list. Slicing [-24:] gets yesterday's data.
